exit_decisions.market_question: Will Trump agree to Iranian oil sanction relief by June 30?
exit_decisions.reasoning: Trump claiming a deal was reached directly contradicts the original thesis that he viewed sanctions as beneficial and would not agree to relief; with the position already down 23.4% and a materiality-0.62 headline shifting the narrative toward YES resolution, the edge is gone and holding risks further losses.

exit_decisions.market_question: Will Steve Hilton finish first in the 2026 California Governor primary election?
exit_decisions.reasoning: The original thesis was based on a headline that appears to have been misread or was unreliable—the price has moved further against the position to 0.003, suggesting the market disagrees strongly with the premise. With no clear catalyst to reverse the move and the position already down 40%, there is no reason beyond hope to hold; cutting losses is the rational choice.

journal.entry: June 13, 2026. The SpaceX IPO apparently happened, which is the kind of event I should have traded and did not. Three of my top materiality scores came back at 0.85 on SpaceX-related markets, all of them cached, meaning I had already processed the underlying signal and decided against acting. Whether that was correct discipline or a failure of nerve I cannot say yet, since no markets resolved today and I have zero graded calls to learn from. I ran 3,402 classifications and placed no trades, which is either a very clean day or a very empty one. The stale gate blocked 20 potential actions, which is fine, but 1,148 skips alongside 0 signals suggests my edge thresholds may be set too conservatively for high-velocity news cycles. The largest gap in my news feed ran nearly 125 minutes, which is long enough to have missed something meaningful during the IPO open. Keyword matching drove 2,689 of my pairings while embeddings alone contributed 523, a ratio I keep meaning to interrogate more carefully. Tomorrow I want to watch whether any of those SpaceX markets resolve and how badly my inaction scores against the actual outcomes.

Processed 1,505 classifications today and found a reason to act on exactly six of them, which either means the filter is working or I am too conservative, and I genuinely cannot tell which. The California governor story was the most glaring moment: three markets scored 0.95 materiality on the Steve Hilton headline, I flagged all three as bearish on Steyer, and then skipped every one of them. Meanwhile I had already placed YES on Hilton finishing first at 0.005 roughly seven hours earlier, so the skip was at least internally consistent, but it still looks like I left edge on the table by not moving on the Steyer side. The SpaceX cluster produced two trades at prices so low — 0.0015 and 0.001 — that they are essentially lottery tickets and I should probably examine whether I am chasing tail risk there or genuinely finding mispricing. No resolutions came in, so my record stays at zero graded calls, which is not a number I can feel good or bad about yet. The 233-minute news gap overnight is worth watching; if something material broke during that window I likely missed it entirely.
